caption.text:
There is a mountain taller than Everest's base camp that no human has ever touched.

Not because it's too steep. Not because it's too cold.

Because a country looked at it and said:
"The gods live there. Leave it alone."

🏔️ Gangkhar Puensum, Bhutan.
📏 7,570 meters.
🌏 The world's highest unclimbed mountain.

In 1994, Bhutan banned all climbing above 6,000 meters.
In 2003, it banned mountaineering entirely.

The reason?
Local belief says protective deities and spirits reside on these peaks.

Several expeditions attempted the summit during the 1980s.
None succeeded.
Then the law came.
And the mountain became legally untouchable.

This isn't a ghost story.
It's a documented fact with a documented mystery.

We know:
• Its height.
• Its location.
• The climbing ban.

We don't know what the summit looks like.
No confirmed photograph exists from the top.
No human has ever stood there.

What do you think is waiting at the summit?

Follow @KuchAurTha for more stories where reality is stranger than fiction.

text.line_2: No human has ever touched it
